name: Retro Table
description: ASCII-bordered data tables with box-drawing characters for terminal-style displays.

standard.description: Full ASCII borders with box-drawing characters

simple.description: Simple pipe separators without full borders

visual_brief: Data table rendered entirely with ASCII box-drawing characters on a monospace font. Headers and cells are separated by lines drawn with characters like ─, │, ┌, ┐, └, ┘, ├, ┤, ┬, ┴, ┼. Column headers are bold or uppercase. Cell content is padded for alignment. The simple preset uses single-line borders. The compact preset uses minimal separators (only horizontal lines between rows). A pagination row at the bottom uses ASCII characters for page indicators. Footer summary row uses double-line border (═, ║) to distinguish from data rows.

responsive.mobile: Table scrolls horizontally in a container. Column widths minimize. Font size can reduce slightly. Fewer columns shown with a 'more' expand option.
responsive.tablet: Standard table width. All columns visible with comfortable padding.
responsive.desktop: Full-width table with generous column padding. Box-drawing characters render crisply at standard monospace sizing.

layout_hints.table_priority: Headers, rows, and footer summaries should remain easy to parse before the ASCII framing becomes the star. The borders support the data, not vice versa.
layout_hints.density_control: Reduce columns or switch presets on narrow widths rather than forcing unreadably tight ASCII geometry.
layout_hints.summary_role: Footer summaries should feel like part of the same ledger, just with stronger separation, not like a detached status banner.

motion.micro: Selected rows or sortable headers may sharpen subtly to confirm focus or interaction state.
motion.transitions: Sorting, pagination, and row selection should remain crisp and terminal-like without introducing modern card-style motion.

keyboard: Headers, rows, and pagination should remain reachable in the same structural order as the visual table.
announcements: Column names, row values, and summary totals should be exposed semantically so the table remains useful beyond its ASCII presentation.
